response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
response_parsed.opinion: To manage and deal with fake content on social media, it's important to be proactive and vigilant in monitoring your accounts. Here are some steps you can take: 1) Use fact-checking tools: There are several fact-checking tools available that can help you identify fake news and content. Some popular ones include Snopes, FactCheck.org, and PolitiFact. 2) Be cautious of sensational headlines: Fake news often has sensational headlines that are designed to grab your attention. Be skeptical of headlines that seem too good (or bad) to be true. 3) Verify the source: Before sharing any content, make sure it's from a credible source. Check the website's domain name and see if it's a reputable news organization. 4) Use social media platforms' built-in safety features: Many social media platforms have built-in safety features that can help you identify and report fake content.
